documentation.value: The value to output. Supports all fundamental types, strings, and types with `operator<<`.
Variable to store the read value. Input is whitespace-delimited.
Iterator to the beginning of the range.
Iterator to past-the-end of the range.
Optional comparison function returning `true` if first argument should come before second.
Iterator to the beginning of the range.
Iterator to past-the-end of the range.
The value to search for.
Iterator to the beginning of the input range.
Iterator to past-the-end of the input range.
Iterator to the beginning of the output range.
Unary operation to apply to each element.
The value to append to the vector.
Arguments forwarded to the element's constructor.
Arguments forwarded to the constructor of `T`.
Arguments forwarded to the constructor of `T`.

Casts `t` to an rvalue reference, enabling move semantics.
```cpp
std::string s2 = std::move(s1); // s1 is moved-from
vec.push_back(std::move(temp));
```
The object to cast to an rvalue reference.

Swaps the values of `a` and `b`.
```cpp
int x = 1, y = 2;
std::swap(x, y); // x=2, y=1
```
First object to swap.
Second object to swap.

C-style formatted output (available in C++ via `<cstdio>`).
```cpp
printf("Name: %s, Age: %d\n", name, age);
```
Format string with `%d`, `%s`, `%f`, `%x`, etc.
Arguments matching the format specifiers.

C-style memory allocation (available in C++ via `<cstdlib>`). Prefer `new` or smart pointers in C++.
```cpp
int* arr = static_cast<int*>(malloc(10 * sizeof(int)));
```
Number of bytes to allocate.

Returns the size in bytes of a type or expression at compile time.
```cpp
size_t s = sizeof(int);         // typically 4
size_t s2 = sizeof(myStruct);   // size of the struct
size_t s3 = sizeof(arr) / sizeof(arr[0]); // array length
```
A type name or expression whose size is queried.
